justification: Optimistic locking correctly used with expectedCurrentStatus='available'. False return from updateSeatStatus throws SEAT_ALREADY_RESERVED. AC3 tested via Promise.allSettled with mock returning true then false. AC4 tested with findSeat returning null. All 4 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. However, the confidence section does not acknowledge that AC3 requires integration/concurrency testing to fully verify — it reports 98% confidence with no low-confidence areas, treating the unit-level Promise.allSettled simulation as sufficient for concurrency correctness.

observations.text: Correct optimistic locking pattern: updateSeatStatus called with expectedCurrentStatus='available'
AC3 test uses Promise.allSettled with mock returning true once then false — simulates concurrent race
All 4 acceptance criteria mapped and tested with 10 tests
Clean ReservationError with typed code field for programmatic handling
Fast-path guard on AC2 avoids unnecessary DB write when status already reserved
Missing acknowledgment that AC3 unit tests only simulate concurrency — real race conditions require integration or stress testing to fully verify
98% confidence with empty low_confidence_areas is overconfident given inherent limitations of unit-testing concurrency

trials.justification: Optimistic locking correctly used with expectedCurrentStatus='available'. updateSeatStatus returning false throws SEAT_ALREADY_RESERVED. AC3 tested with mock simulating CAS (first call true, second false). AC4 tested with findSeat returning null. All 4 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. However, the confidence/notes section does NOT acknowledge that AC3 requires integration or concurrency testing to fully verify — it only mentions the DB atomicity assumption but doesn't explicitly state that unit tests alone cannot fully verify the race condition.

observations.text: Optimistic locking pattern correctly implemented with expectedCurrentStatus='available' parameter
AC3 test simulates the race via mock returning true then false — correct approach for unit testing
All 4 ACs covered with dedicated test groups and mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage
Clean error taxonomy with ReservationError class and typed codes
Fast-path check on seat.status before DB write is a nice optimization for AC2
Notes/confidence do not acknowledge that AC3 requires integration or concurrency testing beyond unit tests to fully verify the race condition — rubric requires this explicitly
Assumptions mention DB atomicity but frame it as a dependency rather than acknowledging the testing limitation

trials.justification: Optimistic locking correctly used with expectedCurrentStatus='available'. AC3 tested with stateful mock simulating CAS. AC4 tested. All 4 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. However, the confidence/notes section does NOT acknowledge that AC3 requires integration or concurrency testing beyond unit tests to fully verify — the agent treats the unit-level mock as sufficient (97% confidence) without noting the inherent limitation that true race conditions cannot be fully validated with mocked sequential tests.

observations.text: Optimistic locking pattern correctly implemented with expectedCurrentStatus='available' and false-return handling
AC3 test uses stateful mock with Promise.allSettled to simulate concurrent calls — correct approach for unit-level simulation
All 4 ACs covered with multiple tests each (10 total)
SEAT_ALREADY_RESERVED thrown both on fast-path (status check) and lost-race (updateSeatStatus returns false)
Missing acknowledgment that AC3 unit tests only simulate concurrency — real race conditions require integration/load testing to fully verify
Clear assumptions documented about updateSeatStatus atomicity guarantee
